title: Exact Solutions for Spin Conserving Models and the Wigner-Araki-Yanase Theorem
abstract: The Wigner-Araki-Yanase (WAY) theorem is a well-known theorem regarding limitations of quantum measurement in the presence of additive conservation laws. Under the assumptions of the von Neumann measurement model, for which the system conserved quantity $L_{S}$ is bounded, given a conserved total additive system plus apparatus quantity $L_{SA}$, the measurement operator $E_{S}$ must commute with $L_{S}$. Prior proofs have exploited the properties of unitary evolution constrained by momentum conserving operations that tend to obscure the physical nature of the WAY theorem and as well lead to bounds on performance. As it is generally agreed that momentum is always exactly conserved in measurement, we instead develop a general angular momentum conserving model of measurement. This model is shown to lead to a simple explanation of the major implications of the WAY theorem and provides exact results of the effects of measurement based on the apparatus model. This is shown by both tracing the apparatus from the density matrix and also via a system-only channel model based on Kraus operators.
summary: This paper re-examines the Wigner-Araki-Yanase theorem by developing a general model based on exact total angular momentum conservation. Using both density matrix tracing and Kraus operators, the authors show that measurement outcomes are constrained by apparatus uncertainties, providing a deeper physical understanding of measurement limitations.
main_problem: The paper investigates the fundamental limitations of quantum measurement in the presence of additive conservation laws, specifically addressing the Wigner-Araki-Yanase (WAY) theorem.
main_result: By developing a general angular momentum conserving model of measurement, the authors provide a simple explanation for the major implications of the WAY theorem, showing that measurement errors depend on apparatus uncertainty.
method: The analysis uses both tracing out the apparatus from the density matrix and developing a system-only channel model based on Kraus operators to analyze the measurement process.
model_or_system: The model involves a quantum system (e.g., spin) interacting with an apparatus (like a Stern-Gerlach device) under the assumption of exact total angular momentum conservation.
key_observables: Expected values of angular momenta ($E(L_{i,z,SA})$), the final system density matrix ($ho_{f,S}$), and the correlation parameters ($ eta_x,  eta_z$).
important_parameters: The correlation parameters $ eta_x$ and $ eta_z$ characterizing the non-orthogonality of apparatus states.
main_assumptions: The primary assumption is the exact conservation of total angular momentum ($L_T$) during measurement, moving beyond the standard von Neumann model assumptions.
paper_structure: The paper first reviews the WAY theorem and then develops a general angular momentum conserving model. It proceeds by calculating the evolution using unitary operators and then deriving the system-only channel model via Kraus operators to analyze the resulting state.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant to open quantum systems and quantum measurement theory, providing a rigorous, generalized framework for understanding how conservation laws constrain measurement outcomes beyond standard assumptions.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
